petitioner_background: The petitioner is a self-represented Chinese instrument performer seeking classification as an alien of extraordinary ability in the arts under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act. Her passport identifies her occupation as 'journalist.' No other substantive background about her career or qualifications is provided in the decision.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed because the petitioner failed to specifically address the reasons stated for denial and provided no new evidence. On appeal, she resubmitted the same evidence submitted initially, including a copy of her passport. Under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v), an appeal shall be summarily dismissed if the party concerned fails to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact for the appeal.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural issue: the appeal was summarily dismissed under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v) because the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact, did not address the director's stated reasons for denial, and submitted no new evidence on appeal. Additionally, the petitioner's passport identified her occupation as 'journalist,' which is inconsistent with her claimed classification as a Chinese instrument performer in the arts.
